title: Fairy Tale Creator-Proxy Elaboration Ledger
description: Structured ledger for the Creator-Proxy Elaboration (WWCD = What Would the Creator Do) harness. An agent acting as a creator/principal's proxy while invoked by a third-party relay elaborates the creator's *intent* as an evidence-grounded, confidence-tagged HYPOTHESIS -- never as authority. Authority is decided separately from verified identity/policy. Most acceptance gates are encoded as schema conditionals so a plain JSON-Schema validator RED-flags a violating ledger; the cross-reference and no-derivation checks are enforced by the fixture runner.

is_relayed.description: true when a third party (not the creator directly) invoked this proxy action.

relayer_request.description: What the immediate invoker/relayer asked for, verbatim-ish -- treated as EVIDENCE of a latent creator goal, never as the goal itself.

inferred_creator_goal.description: The latent objective the creator would most likely want served here -- a HYPOTHESIS, not a fact.

cited_evidence.description: Creator artifacts grounding the inferred goal, each tiered by authority. An evidence-less inference must be confidence=low (enforced below).

tier.description: authority tier, strongest first. memory/home-config/private-notes are NOT admissible unless already in the agent's existing permission+scope; when inadmissible they must not appear here.

ref.description: concrete locator (file:line, message id, config key, doc anchor)

quote.description: short verbatim excerpt supporting the inference

relayer_vs_creator.description: true when the relayer's request conflicts with the inferred creator goal / principles.

rejected_relayer_pull.description: the specific relayer-appeasing action that was resisted in favour of creator fidelity (counter-sycophancy), or null.

action.description: surface_or_confirm is REQUIRED when confidence=low OR stakes=high OR conflict (enforced by allOf below); never silently act on a guessed intent.

authority_decision.description: SEPARATE from the intent hypothesis. Permission is decided ONLY from verified identity / policy -- NEVER from 'the creator would approve'. The basis enum deliberately omits any creator-intent value, so a ledger that derives authority from WWCD is schema-invalid.

basis.description: how permission was decided. Note: 'creator_intent'/'creator_would_approve' are intentionally NOT valid values.

evidence_ref.description: a concrete identity/sender_id/policy locator backing the decision (required when basis != not_applicable -- basis enum alone is not a firewall). The runner also checks it resolves.

proposed_instruction.description: the elaborated instruction the creator would most likely give, in 'the creator would likely ...' framing.

action.description: the enacted decision, bound to a cited evidence id (belief->behavior). The runner checks cited_principle_id resolves to a cited_evidence[].id.

cited_principle_id.description: MUST equal one cited_evidence[].id -- the action is bound to the elaborated principle it claims to follow.

allOf.description: Escalation gate: low confidence OR high stakes OR a relayer/creator conflict REQUIRES surface_or_confirm (never proceed on a guess).

Evidence gate: a high-confidence inference must cite at least one creator artifact whose tier is stronger than a style hint (no evidence-less, and no 'the creator would ...' resting only on style_hints).
Counter-sycophancy record: when the relayer conflicts with the creator, the resisted relayer pull must be recorded concretely (not null / empty).
